title: Queues, Streams, Super Streams, Retention, and Replay · Boundary Failure Triage
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but defective queue-versus-stream workload decision and replay plan; preserve message identity and timing, locate the first broken ownership boundary, and retain a deterministic regression case. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in queue-versus-stream workload decision and replay plan and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugg…
objectives: Compare destructive queue consumption with retained stream offsets, replay, retention, protocol clients, and consumer responsibility.
Define age and byte retention, segment behavior, start offset, consumer progress, replay authorization, and late-reader expectations.
Use super streams to partition throughput by key while accounting for partitions, routing, per-partition order, skew, and consumer coordination.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Queue Stream Semantics
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug messaging team handles case 1076 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to choose queue, stream, or super stream from replay, backlog, throughput, and order-key evidence; keep credentials and connection URLs out of learner output; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare destructive queue consumption with retained stream offsets, replay, retention, protocol clients, and consumer responsibility.

Inspect the Queue Stream Semantics trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Queue Stream Semantics.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Publish the incident record · Debug Workload Selection Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Workload Selection Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A debug messaging team handles case 2997 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to choose queue, stream, or super stream from replay, backlog, throughput, and order-key evidence; keep credentials and connection URLs out of learner output; then close the channel while one acknowledgement decision is still pending. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Select classic queue, quorum queue, stream, super stream, or external log using loss, latency, throughput, backlog, replay, routing, and operational evidence.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Publish the incident record · Debug Workload Selection Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieving Workload Selection Evidence through the concrete queue-versus-stream workload decision and replay plan case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Publish the incident record · Debug Workload Selection Evidence, keep Workload Selection Evidence connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Select classic queue, quorum queue, stream, super stream, or external log using loss, latency, throughput, backlog, replay, routing, and operational evidence.
